question: Trace a plausible kill-chain progression an attacker may follow across the phases initial access -> execution -> persistence -> lateral movement.

hops.observation: During Initial Access, the adversary uses Cloud Accounts (T1078.004).
hops.inference: Valid accounts in cloud environments may allow adversaries to perform actions to achieve Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.

hops.observation: During Execution, the adversary uses Software Deployment Tools (T1072).
hops.inference: Adversaries may gain access to and use centralized software suites installed within an enterprise to execute commands and move laterally through the network.

hops.observation: During Persistence, the adversary uses Cloud Accounts (T1078.004).
hops.inference: Valid accounts in cloud environments may allow adversaries to perform actions to achieve Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.

hops.observation: During Lateral Movement, the adversary uses Remote Services (T1021).
hops.inference: Adversaries may use [Valid Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078) to log into a service that accepts remote connections, such as telnet, SSH, and VNC.

conclusion: Each phase relies on the prior foothold; defenders should detect and disrupt the earliest hop to break the chain.

question: Trace a plausible kill-chain progression an attacker may follow across the phases reconnaissance -> initial access -> execution -> exfiltration.

hops.observation: During Reconnaissance, the adversary uses Spearphishing Link (T1598.003).
hops.inference: Adversaries may send spearphishing messages with a malicious link to elicit sensitive information that can be used during targeting.

hops.observation: During Initial Access, the adversary uses Cloud Accounts (T1078.004).
hops.inference: Valid accounts in cloud environments may allow adversaries to perform actions to achieve Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.

hops.observation: During Execution, the adversary uses Software Deployment Tools (T1072).
hops.inference: Adversaries may gain access to and use centralized software suites installed within an enterprise to execute commands and move laterally through the network.

hops.observation: During Exfiltration, the adversary uses Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol (T1048).
hops.inference: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol than that of the existing command and control channel.

conclusion: Each phase relies on the prior foothold; defenders should detect and disrupt the earliest hop to break the chain.

question: Trace a plausible kill-chain progression an attacker may follow across the phases credential access -> lateral movement -> collection -> exfiltration.

hops.observation: During Credential Access, the adversary uses LSASS Memory (T1003.001).
hops.inference: Adversaries may attempt to access credential material stored in the process memory of the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS).

hops.observation: During Lateral Movement, the adversary uses Remote Services (T1021).
hops.inference: Adversaries may use [Valid Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078) to log into a service that accepts remote connections, such as telnet, SSH, and VNC.

hops.observation: During Collection, the adversary uses Data from Cloud Storage (T1530).
hops.inference: Adversaries may access data from cloud storage.

hops.observation: During Exfiltration, the adversary uses Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol (T1048).
hops.inference: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol than that of the existing command and control channel.

conclusion: Each phase relies on the prior foothold; defenders should detect and disrupt the earliest hop to break the chain.

question: Trace a plausible kill-chain progression an attacker may follow across the phases execution -> privilege escalation -> defense evasion -> impact.

hops.observation: During Execution, the adversary uses Software Deployment Tools (T1072).
hops.inference: Adversaries may gain access to and use centralized software suites installed within an enterprise to execute commands and move laterally through the network.

hops.observation: During Privilege Escalation, the adversary uses Cloud Accounts (T1078.004).
hops.inference: Valid accounts in cloud environments may allow adversaries to perform actions to achieve Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.

hops.observation: During Defense Evasion, the adversary uses Patch System Image (T1601.001).
hops.inference: Adversaries may modify the operating system of a network device to introduce new capabilities or weaken existing defenses.(Citation: Killing the myth of Cisco IOS rootkits) (Citation: Killing IOS diversity myth) (Citation: Cisco IOS Shellco

hops.observation: During Impact, the adversary uses Service Stop (T1489).
hops.inference: Adversaries may stop or disable services on a system to render those services unavailable to legitimate users.

conclusion: Each phase relies on the prior foothold; defenders should detect and disrupt the earliest hop to break the chain.
